sql_array: CREATE TABLE example_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO example_table (id, name, description) VALUES (1, '   Test User   ', 'Sample description with SPARQL type: <Class>');
SELECT id, rtrim(name) AS trimmed_name FROM example_table WHERE name a <Class>;
SELECT id, rtrim(description) AS trimmed_desc FROM example_table WHERE rtrim(name) a <Class> AND description IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM example_table GROUP BY rtrim(description) HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ref_id INT, FOREIGN KEY(ref_id) REFERENCES example_table(id));
INSERT INTO another_table (id, ref_id) VALUES (1, 1), (2, NULL);
SELECT a.id, rtrim(a.name) AS cleaned_name, b.ref_id FROM example_table a LEFT JOIN another_table b ON a.id = COALESCE(b.ref_id, -1) WHERE a.name a <Class>;
SELECT COUNT(*), rtrim(name) FROM example_table GROUP BY rtrim(name) HAVING rtrim(name) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT rtrim(name) AS trimmed_name, MIN(id) FROM example_table GROUP BY trimmed_name ORDER BY MIN(id) DESC;
